title: Ceratostema ellipticum

This family is part of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the family Gesneriaceae, Ceratostema ellipticum is classified as a genus of flowering plants native to the Andes mountains. The genus Ceratostema is further divided into several species, with Ceratostema ellipticum being one of them. intro: Ceratostema ellipticum, also known as Cat's Claw, is a woody vine native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Ceratostema ellipticum include alkaloids, particularly oxindole alkaloids, including isovincamine and vincamine, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Cat's Claw is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules, as well as being incorporated into traditional Amazonian remedies for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
